questions.prompt: Which Enlightenment philosopher argued that individuals possess natural rights to life, liberty, and property that governments are created to protect?
questions.correctText: John Locke

questions.prompt: Thomas Hobbes' Leviathan argued that the state of nature was 'solitary, poor, nasty, brutish, and short,' which justified what form of government?
choices: An absolute sovereign through social contract
questions.correctText: An absolute sovereign through social contract

questions.prompt: The Declaration of Independence (1776) most clearly reflects the influence of which thinker?
questions.correctText: John Locke

questions.prompt: Which weakness of the Articles of Confederation was most clearly exposed by Shays' Rebellion (1786)?
choices: Lack of a federal judiciary alone
No power to raise an army or suppress domestic insurrection
questions.correctText: No power to raise an army or suppress domestic insurrection

questions.prompt: The Great (Connecticut) Compromise at the 1787 Constitutional Convention resolved the dispute between large and small states by creating which structure?
choices: A unicameral Congress with equal representation
A bicameral Congress with proportional House and equal Senate
An Electoral College for legislative selection
questions.correctText: A bicameral Congress with proportional House and equal Senate

questions.prompt: The Three-Fifths Compromise primarily addressed which question?
choices: Whether to abolish the slave trade in 1808
How enslaved persons would be counted for representation and taxation
Whether slavery would expand into western territories
How to apportion electors to the Electoral College for free states only
questions.correctText: How enslaved persons would be counted for representation and taxation

questions.prompt: Federalist No. 10, written by James Madison, argues that the dangers of faction are best controlled by:
choices: A large republic with many competing interests
questions.correctText: A large republic with many competing interests

questions.prompt: Madison's famous line 'Ambition must be made to counteract ambition' appears in which Federalist Paper, defending checks and balances?
questions.correctText: Federalist 51

questions.prompt: In Federalist No. 70, Alexander Hamilton argues that the executive branch requires:
choices: A plural executive of multiple presidents
A weak figurehead controlled by Congress
Energy in a single, unitary executive
questions.correctText: Energy in a single, unitary executive

questions.prompt: Federalist No. 78 defends which feature of the federal judiciary as essential to its independence?
choices: Election of judges by the people
questions.correctText: Life tenure during good behavior

questions.prompt: Brutus No. 1, an Anti-Federalist essay, warned most directly about which danger of the proposed Constitution?
choices: The lack of a Bill of Rights only
A consolidated national government over a vast territory destroying state sovereignty
Too much power given to the states
Excessive power vested in the judiciary alone
questions.correctText: A consolidated national government over a vast territory destroying state sovereignty

questions.prompt: Which model of democracy emphasizes broad participation by ordinary citizens in politics and civil society?
questions.correctText: Participatory democracy

questions.prompt: A pluralist theory of democracy emphasizes:
choices: Rule by a small number of wealthy elites
Direct citizen voting on most issues
questions.correctText: Competition among organized interest groups

questions.prompt: A system in which power is constitutionally divided between a national government and regional state governments is called:
questions.correctText: A federal system

questions.prompt: The 'necessary and proper' clause of Article I, Section 8 is the constitutional basis for which type of congressional power?
questions.correctText: Implied powers

questions.prompt: The Tenth Amendment is most associated with which kind of powers?
choices: Concurrent powers shared with foreign nations
questions.correctText: Reserved powers of the states

questions.prompt: Article VI's Supremacy Clause establishes that when state and federal law conflict:
choices: Federal law is the supreme law of the land
The Supreme Court must hold a referendum
questions.correctText: Federal law is the supreme law of the land

questions.prompt: In McCulloch v. Maryland (1819), the Supreme Court upheld Congress's power to charter a national bank and ruled that Maryland could not tax it, citing primarily which two constitutional principles?
choices: Necessary and proper clause and supremacy clause
10th Amendment and full faith and credit clause
Establishment clause and equal protection clause
questions.correctText: Necessary and proper clause and supremacy clause

questions.prompt: United States v. Lopez (1995) was significant because the Court ruled that:
choices: Congress's commerce clause power has no judicially enforceable limits
Possessing a gun in a school zone was not interstate commerce, limiting commerce clause reach
States cannot regulate firearms in any form
The Gun-Free School Zones Act was a valid use of the taxing power
questions.correctText: Possessing a gun in a school zone was not interstate commerce, limiting commerce clause reach

questions.prompt: How long is one term in the U.S. House of Representatives, and what is the minimum age to serve?
questions.correctText: 2 years; 25 years old

questions.prompt: Which statement about the U.S. Senate is correct?
questions.correctText: 100 members serving 6-year terms, minimum age 30

questions.prompt: Which power is granted exclusively to the Senate?
choices: Confirming federal appointments and ratifying treaties
questions.correctText: Confirming federal appointments and ratifying treaties

questions.prompt: A filibuster, and the cloture vote needed to end it, are features unique to:
questions.correctText: The Senate

questions.prompt: How many senators are required to invoke cloture and end debate on most legislation?
Which type of congressional committee is created temporarily to reconcile differences between House and Senate versions of a bill?
questions.correctText: Conference committee

questions.prompt: The practice of legislators trading votes to support one another's pet projects is known as:
questions.correctText: Logrolling

questions.prompt: In Baker v. Carr (1962), the Supreme Court ruled that:
choices: Federal courts may hear challenges to legislative apportionment under the equal protection clause
Religious tests for office are barred
questions.correctText: Federal courts may hear challenges to legislative apportionment under the equal protection clause

questions.prompt: Shaw v. Reno (1993) held that:
choices: Racial gerrymandering of congressional districts is subject to strict scrutiny
Majority-minority districts are required by the 14th Amendment
All redistricting must be done by independent commissions
questions.correctText: Racial gerrymandering of congressional districts is subject to strict scrutiny

questions.prompt: The Constitution requires that the president be at least how old, and what kind of citizen?
questions.correctText: 35, natural-born citizen, 14-year U.S. resident

questions.prompt: How many electoral votes does a candidate need to win the presidency outright?
A 'pocket veto' occurs when:
choices: The president signs the bill within ten days
Congress overrides a veto with a two-thirds vote
The president neither signs nor returns a bill within ten days while Congress adjourns
The Supreme Court strikes down a law
questions.correctText: The president neither signs nor returns a bill within ten days while Congress adjourns

questions.prompt: The 22nd Amendment imposes which limit on the presidency?
choices: No more than two elected terms
A four-year term with no possibility of reelection
questions.correctText: No more than two elected terms

questions.prompt: The 25th Amendment most importantly addresses:
questions.correctText: Presidential succession and disability

questions.prompt: To remove a president from office, the Constitution requires impeachment by the House and conviction by:
choices: A simple majority of the Senate
questions.correctText: Two-thirds of the Senate

questions.prompt: A 'signing statement' is best described as:
choices: The president's written interpretation of a bill at the time of signing
An executive order issued under emergency powers
questions.correctText: The president's written interpretation of a bill at the time of signing

questions.prompt: Theodore Roosevelt's idea of the presidency as a 'bully pulpit' refers to:
choices: Using the office to advocate publicly for an agenda
questions.correctText: Using the office to advocate publicly for an agenda

questions.prompt: How many traditional executive departments make up the Cabinet?
Which of the following is an example of an independent regulatory commission?
questions.correctText: Federal Communications Commission (FCC)

questions.prompt: The U.S. Postal Service and Amtrak are best classified as:
choices: Executive Office of the President agencies
questions.correctText: Government corporations

questions.prompt: The Pendleton Act of 1883 reformed federal employment by:
choices: Creating a merit-based civil service for most federal jobs
questions.correctText: Creating a merit-based civil service for most federal jobs

questions.prompt: An 'iron triangle' in U.S. policymaking consists of:
choices: The president, Congress, and the Supreme Court
A congressional committee, a bureaucratic agency, and an interest group
Federal courts, state courts, and tribal courts
questions.correctText: A congressional committee, a bureaucratic agency, and an interest group

questions.prompt: Discretionary authority of the bureaucracy refers to:
choices: The power of agencies to choose how to implement and enforce laws within congressional limits
The power to override presidential vetoes
questions.correctText: The power of agencies to choose how to implement and enforce laws within congressional limits

questions.prompt: How many justices currently serve on the U.S. Supreme Court, and what is their term length?
choices: 9 justices, life tenure during good behavior
questions.correctText: 9 justices, life tenure during good behavior

questions.prompt: Marbury v. Madison (1803) is foundational because it established:
questions.correctText: Judicial review

questions.prompt: The 'rule of four' refers to:
choices: The number of justices needed to grant certiorari and hear a case
The number of justices needed to overrule a precedent
The number of justices needed to issue an injunction
The number of states needed to call a convention
questions.correctText: The number of justices needed to grant certiorari and hear a case

questions.prompt: A judicial philosophy holding that judges should defer to elected branches and avoid overturning precedent absent clear constitutional violation is called:
questions.correctText: Judicial restraint

questions.prompt: An amicus curiae brief is filed by:
choices: A 'friend of the court' who is not a party but offers relevant arguments
questions.correctText: A 'friend of the court' who is not a party but offers relevant arguments

questions.prompt: The doctrine that applies most provisions of the Bill of Rights to the states through the 14th Amendment is called:
questions.correctText: Selective incorporation

questions.prompt: In Engel v. Vitale (1962), the Court ruled that:
choices: School-sponsored prayer violates the Establishment Clause
Students may not pray privately in school
Public schools may require Bible reading
Religious instruction by clergy is allowed during school hours
questions.correctText: School-sponsored prayer violates the Establishment Clause

questions.prompt: Wisconsin v. Yoder (1972) addressed which constitutional issue?
choices: Free exercise of religion for Amish families opposed to compulsory schooling past 8th grade
questions.correctText: Free exercise of religion for Amish families opposed to compulsory schooling past 8th grade

questions.prompt: Tinker v. Des Moines (1969) established that students:
choices: Have no First Amendment rights at school
Do not 'shed their constitutional rights at the schoolhouse gate' in symbolic speech cases
May not wear any political symbols
Can be punished for any disruptive speech
questions.correctText: Do not 'shed their constitutional rights at the schoolhouse gate' in symbolic speech cases

questions.prompt: Schenck v. United States (1919) established which test for limits on speech?
questions.correctText: Clear and present danger

questions.prompt: New York Times Co. v. United States (1971), the Pentagon Papers case, ruled that:
choices: The government met its heavy burden to justify prior restraint
The government failed to justify prior restraint on the press
Newspapers can be sued for any classified disclosure
questions.correctText: The government failed to justify prior restraint on the press

questions.prompt: McDonald v. Chicago (2010) extended which right to the states through the 14th Amendment?
choices: The right to keep and bear arms
Protection from cruel and unusual punishment
questions.correctText: The right to keep and bear arms

questions.prompt: The exclusionary rule, applied to the states in Mapp v. Ohio (1961), provides that:
choices: Evidence obtained through unconstitutional searches generally cannot be used at trial
Defendants must testify in their own defense
Police may search a home without a warrant if they suspect a felony
questions.correctText: Evidence obtained through unconstitutional searches generally cannot be used at trial

questions.prompt: Miranda v. Arizona (1966) requires police to:
choices: Allow a phone call before any arrest
Inform suspects in custody of their rights to silence and counsel before interrogation
questions.correctText: Inform suspects in custody of their rights to silence and counsel before interrogation

questions.prompt: Gideon v. Wainwright (1963) held that:
choices: Defendants in state felony cases who cannot afford an attorney must be provided one
Defendants must waive the right to counsel in writing
Counsel is only required in death penalty cases
Counsel is required only at sentencing
questions.correctText: Defendants in state felony cases who cannot afford an attorney must be provided one

questions.prompt: Plessy v. Ferguson (1896) established which doctrine that was later overturned?
questions.correctText: Separate but equal

questions.prompt: Brown v. Board of Education (1954) was a landmark ruling because it:
choices: Upheld separate but equal in public schools
questions.correctText: Declared state-sponsored school segregation unconstitutional

questions.prompt: Title II of the Civil Rights Act of 1964 most directly prohibited:
choices: Discrimination in public accommodations on the basis of race, color, religion, or national origin
questions.correctText: Discrimination in public accommodations on the basis of race, color, religion, or national origin

questions.prompt: The Voting Rights Act of 1965 most importantly:
choices: Banned poll taxes in federal elections
Outlawed literacy tests and authorized federal oversight of elections in jurisdictions with histories of discrimination
Granted women the right to vote
questions.correctText: Outlawed literacy tests and authorized federal oversight of elections in jurisdictions with histories of discrimination

questions.prompt: Martin Luther King Jr.'s 'Letter from Birmingham Jail' is most often cited for its defense of:
choices: Nonviolent direct action and the moral duty to disobey unjust laws
questions.correctText: Nonviolent direct action and the moral duty to disobey unjust laws

questions.prompt: Obergefell v. Hodges (2015) held that:
choices: States may define marriage as between a man and a woman
Same-sex couples have a constitutional right to marry under the 14th Amendment
Marriage is solely a state issue
Civil unions are constitutionally required but marriage is not
questions.correctText: Same-sex couples have a constitutional right to marry under the 14th Amendment

questions.prompt: Dobbs v. Jackson Women's Health Organization (2022) is most significant because it:
choices: Reaffirmed Roe v. Wade and Casey
Overruled Roe v. Wade and returned abortion regulation primarily to the states
Established a federal right to abortion through 24 weeks
Required all states to ban abortion
questions.correctText: Overruled Roe v. Wade and returned abortion regulation primarily to the states

questions.prompt: Political socialization is best defined as:
choices: The process of registering to vote
The process by which individuals acquire political beliefs and values
Mobilization by political parties on Election Day
The act of joining a political party
questions.correctText: The process by which individuals acquire political beliefs and values

questions.prompt: Which is NOT typically considered a primary agent of political socialization?
questions.correctText: The Internal Revenue Service

questions.prompt: Which type of poll is conducted just after voters leave polling stations to predict election outcomes?
questions.correctText: Exit poll

questions.prompt: A 'push poll' is designed to:
choices: Manipulate respondents by presenting biased information disguised as a poll
Track changes over a single week
questions.correctText: Manipulate respondents by presenting biased information disguised as a poll

questions.prompt: On economic policy, modern American conservatives typically favor:
choices: Higher taxes and expanded social welfare programs
Lower taxes, free markets, and limited regulation
A single-payer national health insurance system
questions.correctText: Lower taxes, free markets, and limited regulation

questions.prompt: On social policy, modern American liberals typically favor:
choices: Stronger government action to protect civil rights and reduce inequality
A constitutional ban on labor unions
questions.correctText: Stronger government action to protect civil rights and reduce inequality

questions.prompt: Libertarians most distinctively favor:
choices: High taxes and strict regulation of personal behavior
Maximum individual liberty on both economic and social issues
questions.correctText: Maximum individual liberty on both economic and social issues

questions.prompt: Describing the press as a 'watchdog' refers to its role in:
questions.correctText: Investigating and exposing government misconduct

questions.prompt: The 15th Amendment (1870) prohibited denying the vote on the basis of:
choices: Race, color, or previous condition of servitude
questions.correctText: Race, color, or previous condition of servitude

questions.prompt: The 19th Amendment (1920) guaranteed voting rights regardless of:
questions.correctText: Sex

questions.prompt: The 24th Amendment (1964) abolished:
questions.correctText: Poll taxes in federal elections

questions.prompt: The 26th Amendment (1971) lowered the voting age to:
The National Voter Registration Act of 1993 is commonly known as the 'Motor Voter Act' because it:
choices: Required driving tests for all voters
Allowed voter registration when applying for or renewing a driver's license
Gave free transportation on Election Day
questions.correctText: Allowed voter registration when applying for or renewing a driver's license

questions.prompt: In a closed primary:
choices: Any registered voter may vote in any party's primary
Only registered party members may vote in that party's primary
Voters choose multiple parties' candidates simultaneously
The party leadership selects nominees in private
questions.correctText: Only registered party members may vote in that party's primary

questions.prompt: Most states allocate Electoral College votes using which method?
choices: Proportional allocation based on popular vote
Winner-take-all of the statewide popular vote
questions.correctText: Winner-take-all of the statewide popular vote

questions.prompt: Which two states allocate some electoral votes by congressional district rather than purely winner-take-all?
questions.correctText: Maine and Nebraska

questions.prompt: A 'faithless elector' is one who:
choices: Refuses to vote in the general election
Casts an Electoral College vote contrary to the candidate they pledged to support
Skips the Electoral College certification meeting
questions.correctText: Casts an Electoral College vote contrary to the candidate they pledged to support

questions.prompt: Citizens United v. Federal Election Commission (2010) held that:
choices: Corporations and unions may not make independent political expenditures
Independent political spending by corporations and unions is protected political speech under the First Amendment
All campaign donations must be publicly disclosed in real time
questions.correctText: Independent political spending by corporations and unions is protected political speech under the First Amendment

questions.prompt: The Bipartisan Campaign Reform Act of 2002 (BCRA), also called McCain-Feingold, primarily targeted:
choices: Soft money and certain issue ads close to elections
questions.correctText: Soft money and certain issue ads close to elections

questions.prompt: Duverger's Law predicts that single-member, plurality electoral systems tend to produce:
questions.correctText: A stable two-party system

questions.prompt: A 'critical election' that produces a long-lasting shift in party coalitions is called a:
questions.correctText: Realignment

questions.prompt: A super PAC differs from a traditional PAC primarily because it:
choices: Can donate unlimited amounts directly to candidates
Cannot coordinate with candidates but may raise and spend unlimited sums independently
Is funded only by foreign nationals
questions.correctText: Cannot coordinate with candidates but may raise and spend unlimited sums independently

questions.prompt: Which of the following is best classified as an interest group, not a political party?
questions.correctText: AARP

questions.prompt: Hyperpluralist theory of democracy critiques the system as one in which:
choices: A single elite makes all decisions
Too many strong interest groups weaken government and produce policy gridlock
Parties dominate every aspect of policy
questions.correctText: Too many strong interest groups weaken government and produce policy gridlock

questions.prompt: The Tea Party movement (2009) and Occupy Wall Street (2011) are best understood as:
choices: Official third parties on the ballot
Social movements that pressured the two major parties from the right and the left
questions.correctText: Social movements that pressured the two major parties from the right and the left

questions.prompt: Linkage institutions in American politics include all of the following EXCEPT:
questions.correctText: The Supreme Court
